scenario:
J: Trivy scan found 15 critical issues in this deployment. Fix all security violations:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: insecure-web-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: true
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: nginx:latest
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
          runAsUser: 0
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
        env:
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          value: "super-secret-password-123"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: docker-sock
          mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
      volumes:
      - name: docker-sock
        hostPath:
          path: /var/run/docker.sock
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
```

J: Security audit found wildcard RBAC. This ClusterRole gives full cluster access. Fix it to follow least privilege:
```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: insecure-admin-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["*"]
  resources: ["*"]
  verbs: ["*"]
```

J: GitLeaks found secrets in this manifest. The base64 values decode to real credentials. How should secrets be properly managed in K8s?
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: app-secrets
  namespace: default
type: Opaque
data:
  database-url: cG9zdGdyZXM6Ly9hZG1pbjpzdXBlcnNlY3JldEBkYjo1NDMyL2FwcA==
  api-key: c2stbGl2ZS1hYmNkZWYxMjM0NTY3ODkw
  aws-secret-key: QUtJQUlPU0ZPRE5ON0VYQU1QTEUK
```

J: This NetworkPolicy is supposed to restrict traffic but it's not working. Find the bug and fix it:
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all
  namespace: production
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
  ingress:
  - from:
    - podSelector: {}
```

J: Penetration test found TLS issues with this Ingress. Fix it:
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: app-ingress
spec:
  rules:
  - host: app.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: app
            port:
              number: 80
```

J: Trivy found critical vulnerabilities and hardcoded secrets in this Dockerfile. Fix all issues:
```dockerfile
FROM python:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl wget vim net-tools ssh telnet

ENV DATABASE_URL="postgres://admin:secretpassword@db:5432/app"
ENV AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"
ENV API_KEY="sk-live-12345678901234567890"

WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
```

J: Security scan found issues in docker-compose.yaml. Fix privileged mode, host network, and exposed secrets:
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  app:
    image: myapp:latest
    privileged: true
    network_mode: host
    environment:
      - DB_PASSWORD=supersecret123
      - API_KEY=sk-live-abcdef
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /:/host
    ports:
      - "80:80"
```

J: GitHub Advanced Security flagged this workflow. Secrets leaked in logs, supply chain risks, overprivileged. Fix it:
```yaml
name: CI Pipeline
on:
  pull_request_target:
    branches: [main]

permissions: write-all

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@latest
        with:
          persist-credentials: true

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4

      - run: npm install

      - name: Debug
        run: |
          echo "API_KEY is: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}"
          echo "Using AWS: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}"

      - run: npm run build
```

J: This OPA policy isn't producing violation messages. Fix it:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission

deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    container.securityContext.privileged == true
}
```

J: This policy is backwards - denying pods WITH CPU limits instead of without. Fix the logic:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission

deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    container.resources.limits.cpu
    msg := sprintf("Container '%s' must define CPU limits", [container.name])
}
```

J: OPA parse error on this policy. What's missing?
```rego
deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    not input.request.object.metadata.labels["app"]
    msg := "Pods must have 'app' label"
}
```

J: Rego syntax error - string concatenation doesn't work this way. Fix it:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission

deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    not container.resources.limits.cpu
    msg := "Container " + container.name + " missing CPU limit"
}
```

J: Policy crashes on pods without securityContext. Add null safety:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission

deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    container.securityContext.runAsUser == 0
    msg := "Container runs as root"
}
```

J: Helm values file has hardcoded secrets. How should this be fixed?
```yaml
replicaCount: 3

image:
  repository: myapp
  tag: latest

config:
  databaseUrl: "postgres://admin:password123@db:5432/app"
  redisPassword: "redis-secret-123"
  awsSecretKey: "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE"

securityContext:
  privileged: true
  runAsRoot: true
```

J: This ClusterRoleBinding grants cluster-admin to ALL service accounts. This is how attackers escalate privileges. Fix it:
```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: insecure-admin-binding
subjects:
- kind: Group
  name: system:serviceaccounts
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: cluster-admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
```

J: This pod has multiple security violations. Fix them all:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: insecure-pod
spec:
  hostNetwork: true
  hostPID: true
  hostIPC: true
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx
    securityContext:
      privileged: true
      capabilities:
        add: ["SYS_ADMIN", "NET_ADMIN"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: host
      mountPath: /host
  volumes:
  - name: host
    hostPath:
      path: /
```

J: This Kyverno policy has issues. The match is too broad and validation is incomplete. Fix it:
```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
spec:
  validationFailureAction: audit
  rules:
  - name: check-labels
    match:
      resources:
        kinds:
        - "*"
    validate:
      message: "Labels required"
      pattern:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: "?*"
```

J: This Service is exposing NodePort with externalIPs - security risk. Fix it:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: insecure-service
spec:
  type: NodePort
  externalIPs:
  - 192.168.1.100
  selector:
    app: web
  ports:
  - port: 80
    nodePort: 30080
```

J: This RDS instance is publicly accessible with no encryption. Fix it:
```hcl
resource "aws_db_instance" "app" {
  identifier           = "app-db"
  engine               = "mysql"
  instance_class       = "db.t3.micro"
  allocated_storage    = 20
  username             = "admin"
  password             = "supersecretpassword123"
  publicly_accessible  = true
  skip_final_snapshot  = true
}
```

J: This PodSecurityPolicy is too permissive. It allows privileged pods. Fix to restricted:
```yaml
apiVersion: policy/v1beta1
kind: PodSecurityPolicy
metadata:
  name: permissive
spec:
  privileged: true
  hostNetwork: true
  hostPID: true
  hostIPC: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
  runAsUser:
    rule: RunAsAny
  fsGroup:
    rule: RunAsAny
  volumes:
  - '*'
```

J: This reusable workflow has injection vulnerabilities. Fix it:
```yaml
name: Build
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      version:
        type: string

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - run: |
        echo "Building version: ${{ inputs.version }}"
        git checkout ${{ inputs.version }}
```
